concept: Sacred Place / Holy Site / Place of Reverence
description: A place regarded with reverence and respect; a site of spiritual significance. 'Thunepa' names where the sacred dwells—temples, groves, mountains, any place treated with ultimate care.
sound_symbolism: The word honors place. 'Thu' /θu/ begins with reverent breath, sacredness present. 'Ne' settles with grounded presence. Then 'pa' completes with gentle affirmation, sacred place confirmed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'thunepa thiro nai.' (the sacred place is serene). Related to 'thoepa' (sacred) and 'thelui' (sanctuary) but emphasizes location of reverence.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Any place can become sacred—through attention, care, and reverence, the ordinary transforms.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Sacred places anchored communities—sites where the holy was honored, where important rituals occurred.
